description: The Lucchese crime family is one of the Five Families of the American Mafia in New York City. Originating in the early 1920s, it was led by Tommy Gagliano and later Tommy Lucchese, becoming a powerful force in organized crime. The family controls rackets including gambling, loansharking, drug trafficking, and labor racketeering, primarily in New York and New Jersey. It is still active today.

country_links.evidence_quote: the New Jersey faction controlled a $2.2 billion illegal gambling, money laundering and racketeering ring based in New Jersey and Costa Rica.
country_links.context: The Lucchese family's New Jersey faction controlled a gambling, money laundering and racketeering ring based in Costa Rica.

country_links.evidence_quote: In 1946 he attended the Havana Conference in Cuba on behalf of Gagliano.
country_links.context: Lucchese attended the Havana Conference in Cuba on behalf of the family.

country_links.evidence_quote: Eugenio 'Gino' Giannini – was born in 1906 in Calabria . ... In 1942, he was deported to Italy ...
country_links.context: Eugenio Giannini was born in Calabria, Italy, and Dominick Petrilli was deported to Italy.

country_links.evidence_quote: Throughout the mid-1990s, Amuso maintained control of the family from prison... In 1998, as US law enforcement kept pressuring organized crime activities in New York... the FBI captured Amuso in Scranton, Pennsylvania... the FBI captured Casso in Mount Olive, New Jersey... the East Coast of the United States from Springfield, Massachusetts to Southern Florida... Crea gave his approval... to murder an informant in New Hampshire
country_links.context: The Lucchese family operates throughout the United States, including New York, New Jersey, Pennsylvania, Massachusetts, Florida, and New Hampshire.
